fields.name: Koshi Province
fields.description: Home to Mount Everest and the Khumbu region, Koshi Province in eastern Nepal offers some of the world's most iconic trekking routes. From the famous Everest Base Camp trail to the remote Kanchenjunga circuit, this province is a paradise for high-altitude trekkers.

fields.name: Madhesh Province
fields.description: The terai flatlands and foothills of Madhesh Province offer unique wildlife and cultural treks through national parks and indigenous communities, including the famous Parsa Wildlife Reserve corridor.

fields.name: Bagmati Province
fields.description: Surrounding the Kathmandu Valley, Bagmati Province blends ancient cultural heritage with stunning mountain views. Trek through rhododendron forests to viewpoints overlooking the entire Himalayan range.

fields.name: Gandaki Province
fields.description: Gandaki Province is the trekking heartland of Nepal, home to the legendary Annapurna Circuit, Mustang's hidden kingdom, and the sacred lake of Gosaikunda. A diversity of landscapes from subtropical to high alpine awaits.

fields.name: Lumbini Province
fields.description: Birthplace of Lord Buddha, Lumbini Province offers peaceful treks through ancient pilgrimage routes, misty foothills, and serene forests. The Tansen and Palpa regions offer spectacular ridge-top trails with Himalayan vistas.

fields.name: Karnali Province
fields.description: The most remote and wild province of Nepal, Karnali offers pristine wilderness trekking through Rara National Park, Dolpo's blue sheep country, and ancient trade routes. Experience Nepal as it was centuries ago.

fields.name: Sudurpashchim Province
fields.description: Nepal's far-western frontier, Sudurpashchim Province features the dramatic Api-Nampa Conservation Area, Api Himal peaks, and routes through traditional Tharu villages. An off-the-beaten-path trekker's dream.

fields.name: Everest Base Camp Trek
fields.description: The Everest Base Camp trek is one of the world's most iconic journeys. Walking through Sherpa villages, across suspension bridges, and through the Khumbu Valley, you'll witness the majesty of the world's highest peak up close. The trail takes you through Namche Bazaar, Tengboche, Dingboche, and Lobuche before reaching the legendary base camp at 5,364m. The views of Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse, and Ama Dablam will stay with you forever.
fields.short_description: Walk in the footsteps of legends to the base of the world's highest peak — a once-in-a-lifetime Himalayan journey.
fields.highlights:
Namche Bazaar Sherpa village
Tengboche Monastery views
Khumbu Glacier walk
Everest Base Camp at 5,364m
Pangboche and Dingboche acclimatization
Views of Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse
fields.includes:
Domestic flights (Kathmandu–Lukla)
All meals on trail
Teahouse accommodation
Experienced English-speaking guide
National park permits (Sagarmatha)
Porter service
fields.excludes:
International flights
Travel insurance
Personal trekking gear
Tips for guide and porter
Extra drinks and snacks

fields.name: Annapurna Circuit
fields.description: The Annapurna Circuit is widely considered one of the world's greatest treks. Circumnavigating the entire Annapurna massif, this journey takes you through lush subtropical valleys, terraced rice paddies, ancient Gurung villages, alpine meadows, and the dramatic high desert of Mustang. The crossing of Thorong La Pass at 5,416m is the highlight — one of the highest motorable passes in the world, with breathtaking 360-degree views.
fields.short_description: Circumnavigate the entire Annapurna massif through subtropical valleys, ancient villages, and the dramatic Thorong La Pass.
fields.highlights:
Thorong La Pass (5,416m)
Muktinath Temple
Manang village acclimatization
Tatopani hot springs
Poon Hill sunrise
Annapurna Conservation Area
fields.includes:
All teahouse accommodation
Three meals daily
ACCAP permits
Licensed local guide
Porter (1 per 2 trekkers)
fields.excludes:
International & domestic flights
Travel insurance
Personal equipment
Gratuities
Beverages

fields.name: Langtang Valley Trek
fields.description: The Langtang Valley trek offers spectacular scenery without the crowds of the more famous routes. Located north of Kathmandu, the valley is bordered by the Tibetan plateau and features glaciers, yak pastures, and the fascinating Tamang culture. The Langtang National Park is home to red pandas, snow leopards, and Himalayan black bears. Kyanjin Gompa monastery at the trail's end is one of the most atmospheric places in Nepal.
fields.short_description: A stunning valley trek north of Kathmandu through Tamang villages, ancient monasteries, and yak-grazed meadows.
fields.highlights:
Kyanjin Gompa monastery
Langtang Glacier viewpoint
Tserko Ri sunrise (4,984m)
Tamang village culture
Yak cheese factories
Red panda habitat
fields.includes:
Teahouse accommodation
Full board meals
Langtang National Park permit
English-speaking guide
Porter service
fields.excludes:
Kathmandu transfers
Travel insurance
Personal gear
Tips
Alcohol & soft drinks

fields.name: Upper Mustang Trek
fields.description: Upper Mustang, the hidden kingdom of Lo, remained closed to outsiders until 1992 and still requires a special restricted area permit. This otherworldly landscape of eroded ochre cliffs, medieval walled cities, and ancient cave monasteries feels like stepping into another dimension — part Tibetan plateau, part Martian landscape. Lo Manthang, the walled capital, is a living museum of Tibetan Buddhist culture.
fields.short_description: Enter the forbidden kingdom of Lo — a surreal landscape of ochre cliffs, ancient cave monasteries, and Tibetan culture.
fields.highlights:
Lo Manthang walled city
Ancient cave monasteries
Tibetan Buddhist culture
Dramatic eroded cliffs
Chandragiri Pass views
Scenic flight Jomsom-Pokhara
fields.includes:
Restricted Area Permit ($500 included)
All accommodation
Full board meals
Guide & porter
Domestic flight Pokhara-Jomsom
fields.excludes:
International flights
Travel insurance
Personal equipment
Gratuities
Extra snacks

fields.name: Rara Lake Trek
fields.description: Rara Lake, Nepal's largest and deepest lake, sits at 2,990m surrounded by pine and spruce forests in the remote Karnali region. The journey to Rara is an adventure in itself — through terraced fields, small mountain villages unchanged by time, and across high passes with views of the Api and Saipal massifs. The lake changes color through the day from turquoise to deep blue, and its shores are completely free of crowds.
fields.short_description: Trek to Nepal's largest lake in the remote Karnali wilderness — a pristine turquoise gem surrounded by ancient forests.
fields.highlights:
Rara Lake (Nepal's largest lake)
Rara National Park
Red pandas and Himalayan wildlife
Unspoiled Karnali villages
Changeable lake colors
Api and Saipal mountain views
fields.includes:
Domestic flights included
All accommodation (teahouses & camping)
Full board
Guide & porter
National park fees
fields.excludes:
International flights
Travel insurance
Personal gear
Gratuities
Snacks & beverages

fields.name: Gokyo Lakes and Cho La Pass
fields.description: This challenging circuit combines the emerald Gokyo Lakes with the technical Cho La glacier pass for an unforgettable high-altitude adventure in the Khumbu region. Gokyo Ri offers arguably the finest panoramic view in the Himalayas — Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu all in one sweeping vista. The crossing of Cho La Pass at 5,420m requires good fitness and a head for heights.
fields.short_description: Combine Gokyo's emerald lakes with the dramatic Cho La Pass for the finest panoramic Himalayan views.
fields.highlights:
Gokyo Ri summit (5,357m)
Five Gokyo Lakes
Cho La Glacier crossing (5,420m)
Ngozumpa Glacier (Nepal's largest)
Panorama of 4 eight-thousanders
Renjo La Pass option
fields.includes:
Lukla flights
All teahouse accommodation
Full board meals
Experienced high-altitude guide
Porter
All permits
fields.excludes:
International flights
Travel insurance
Personal gear (crampons, ice axe)
Gratuities
Extra food & drinks

fields.name: Poon Hill Trek
fields.description: The Poon Hill trek is the perfect introduction to Nepal's mountains. In just four to five days, you'll walk through rhododendron forests (spectacular in March-April bloom), Gurung villages, and terraced hillsides to reach the iconic Poon Hill viewpoint at sunrise. The panorama reveals Dhaulagiri, Annapurna South, Hiunchuli, Machhapuchhre, and over a dozen other peaks glowing in the morning light — one of the most accessible mountain spectacles on earth.
fields.short_description: The perfect first trek in Nepal — walk through rhododendron forests to Poon Hill's legendary sunrise mountain panorama.
fields.highlights:
Poon Hill sunrise viewpoint
Annapurna & Dhaulagiri panorama
Rhododendron forest trails
Gurung village homestays
Ghorepani village culture
Pokhara city combo possible
fields.includes:
All teahouse accommodation
Full board meals
ACCAP & TIMS permits
English-speaking guide
Porter
fields.excludes:

fields.name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp Trek
fields.description: The Kanchenjunga trek is one of Nepal's most remote and rewarding journeys, visiting the base camps of the world's third-highest peak. The trail passes through diverse vegetation zones from subtropical forests to alpine meadows, and the cultural encounter with indigenous Limbu, Rai, and Sherpa communities is unparalleled. The dual base camps — North at 5,143m and South at 4,600m — offer dramatically different perspectives of the Kanchenjunga massif.
fields.short_description: Trek to the world's third-highest peak through remote wilderness and rich indigenous culture — Nepal's ultimate frontier adventure.
fields.highlights:
Kanchenjunga North Base Camp (5,143m)
Kanchenjunga South Base Camp (4,600m)
Yamphudin village (last outpost)
Limbu and Sherpa culture
Pristine wilderness trails
Kanchenjunga Conservation Area
fields.includes:
All accommodation (mix of teahouses & camping)
Full board
Experienced guide
Two porters
All restricted area permits
fields.excludes:
Domestic flights to Taplejung
International flights
Travel insurance
Camping gear (can be rented)
